briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. As the climate change spokesperson, I will begin today's climate briefing on July 8, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Threats and Extreme Weather Phenomena
sections.content: On July 7, 2026, 'impossible' levels of heatwaves and ocean temperature records were observed globally, once again emphasizing the severity of climate change [26, 46]. Particularly in Europe, a record-breaking heatwave in June resulted in over 2,700 heat-related deaths in France alone, and the 'environmental syndemic' paradox, suggesting that a reduction in fine dust could actually trigger heatwaves, has been raised [3, 47]. These extreme weather phenomena have led to the postponement of the Twin Cities Marathon [27], and warnings that they could cause immense cost losses and damage to infrastructure [37] and roads [40] in New Jersey [28] and the UK. Furthermore, analyses indicate that climate change could lower Lake Powell's water levels in the Western U.S. to a 2026 record low [38], and result in billions of dollars in losses for Hawaii's coral reef tourism [36].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Efforts and Expanding Social Participation
sections.content: Domestically, efforts across society to address climate change are actively continuing, including the announcement of three major climate agendas directly selected by citizens [1]. Yeoncheon County has initiated climate safety education for military personnel and is actively fostering 'Green Soldiers' [8, 16], while various local governments, such as Yeongam County [9, 15] and Gwangmyeong City [14], are raising awareness among future generations through the operation of carbon-neutral agricultural schools and carbon-neutral education. Gurye County has introduced the 'Carbon Moa Tantan-e' system, providing local currency to support carbon-neutral practices [17, 19], and in Wolgot-dong, Siheung City, a resident-led Green Environment Protection Squad has been launched [18], expanding voluntary community participation. Yeoju City Public Health Center has completed providing heating and cooling support to climate-vulnerable groups [2], and Shinseong E&G has exceeded its greenhouse gas reduction targets for three consecutive years [4], demonstrating that businesses, local governments, and civil society are jointly responding to the climate crisis.

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges of Energy Transition and Evaluation of Domestic Climate Policies
sections.content: In discussions on energy sources for the AI era, the argument has been made that nuclear power should be central, supported by renewable energy and LNG [20], however, the controversy surrounding the setback distances for renewable energy facilities remains an unresolved challenge, raising concerns about undermining protections for rural residents [21]. Meanwhile, it has been revealed that the South Korean government self-evaluated over 95% of its national climate crisis adaptation projects implemented over the past five years as 'excellent' [23], leading to 'self-grading' controversy and calls for a thorough review of the policies' effectiveness. This situation underscores the role of price stabilization mechanisms connecting production, stockpiling, and distribution in an era of climate crisis [5], and highlights the importance of research efforts, such as the International Seminar on Climate Change and Soil Ecology [6].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Awareness and Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: The international community is discussing the need to redefine the future-proofing interpretation of the Paris Agreement's goal to limit warming to below 2°C [24], and active research on climate change perception is underway, including studies on Indians' mental imagery of global warming [25] and analyses of public tendencies to under/overestimate participation in climate action [31]. New courses offering optimism and a voice to students amidst the climate crisis are being launched at the University of Toronto [42], and educational and adaptation efforts are continuing, such as Jamaica's transition of climate lessons into long-term climate action [39]. Furthermore, various actors in different countries are responding to climate change, with farmers in India's Bihar region adapting to climate change through traditional crops [43], and AkzoNobel cutting its operational carbon emissions in half four years ahead of its 2030 target [44].

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, climate change is emerging as a global threat, but simultaneously, various response efforts and adaptation attempts are continuing both domestically and internationally. I would like to re-emphasize that citizen participation, scientific approaches, and policy transparency are more crucial than ever in this era of climate crisis. With that, I conclude today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Fine Dust Reduction Leads to Extreme Heatwaves...Europe's 2026 'Environmental Syndemic' Paradox Kang Chan-soo's Ecofile

summary.phenomenon: The 'environmental syndemic' paradox has been raised, suggesting that a reduction in fine dust could lead to extreme heatwaves in Europe by 2026.
summary.cause: The underlying scientific paradox is that a decrease in fine dust reduces the cooling effect of atmospheric aerosols, thereby increasing solar radiation reaching the Earth's surface and potentially intensifying heatwaves.
summary.outlook: Fine dust reduction policies in Europe may unintentionally exacerbate heatwaves.

articles.title: M&S invests in fridges that can cope with weather as hot as 45C
articles.description: Retailer admits it struggled in June heatwave and also had to order more ice-cream to keep pace with demandMarks & Spencer is investing in refrigeration equipment that can cope with weather as hot as 45C as the climate crisis is expected to drive regularly higher temperatures in the UK.“There is no doubt we were struggling in the nine days of [recent] extreme heat,” Stuart Machin, the chief executive of the food, fashion, beauty and homewares retailer, told shareholders at the group’s annual mee

summary.phenomenon: The UK experienced a nine-day extreme heatwave in June, causing operational difficulties for M&S and an increase in ice cream demand.
summary.cause: This is due to the climate crisis being expected to regularly drive higher temperatures in the UK.
summary.outlook: As regularly higher temperatures are anticipated in the UK due to the climate crisis, M&S is investing in refrigeration equipment capable of withstanding temperatures as high as 45C.
